lifecycles._note: Light, VPC-FREE, self-contained. Creates its OWN api (does NOT touch application-apigateway-api-resource) and exercises the apigateway WRITE surface that the base lifecycle leaves uncovered: resource methods, deployments, stages (+config +active-deployment), auths, access-controls, resource-policies, usage-plans (+description +quota) and api-keys (+description). Each family lives in its own optional group keyed `xcov-*` so an environmental 4xx on one create records the CALL for coverage and tears down just that group, never failing the lifecycle. Bodies grounded from docs models (createstage/createmethod/createauth/createaccesscontrols/createusageplan/createapikey/createapideployment/set*) — example values from each model's Example string. PROVENANCE: from docs (best-effort), NOT runtime-proven. Key facts encoded from docs: createapideployment with stage_type=new creates a stage in one call and returns $.deployment_id (api_bodies.json createapideployment); a deployment requires >=1 method to exist first (existing base-lifecycle note: NoMethodsExist) so create-method runs before create-deployment; createstage body needs a real deployment_id (-> uses captured deployment_id); createmethod returns $.methods[] (no id) and methods are addressed by method_type in the path (GET); createauth returns only $.access_token (NO id) so auth_id is recovered via listauths $.auths[0].id; createaccesscontrols returns $.id directly; createusageplan -> $.usage_plan.id; createapikey -> $.api_key.id. Names match pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,48}[a-z0-9]$ (regr{ualpha} is lowercase-alpha, safe). resource-policy body mirrors the base lifecycle's proven shape (placeholder srn -> may 4xx but CALLs setresourcepolicy + deleteresourcepolicy).

steps.name: create-method
steps._note: createmethod (MethodCreateRequestV1Dot3): HTTP integration with a reachable endpoint_url; method_type GET addresses set/delete in the path. Returns $.methods[] -> capture method_type for the set/delete paths (methods are addressed by method_type, not an id). Standalone optional (no group) so a 4xx here does not skip set/delete-method, which then run against the unfilled {method_type} placeholder for coverage.

steps.name: set-method
steps._note: setmethod (MethodSetRequestV1Dot3): query_strings/request_headers required (object[boolean]). Standalone optional: if create-method captured method_type=GET it targets the real method; if create-method failed the unfilled {method_type} placeholder yields a 404 but still CALLs setmethod for coverage.

steps.name: create-deployment
steps._note: createapideployment (api_bodies.json): stage_type=new creates a brand-new stage `dev` in the same call and returns $.deployment_id. Requires >=1 method (created above). deployment_id feeds createstage + setstageactivedeployment.

steps.name: create-stage
steps._note: createstage (StageCreateRequest): stage_name pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,48}[a-z0-9]$ (>=3 chars), deployment_id from the deployment just created. Stage is addressed by stage_name in the path for config/active-deployment/delete.

steps.name: set-stage-config
steps._note: setstageconfiguration (StageSetConfigRequestV1Dot1): cors_enabled + jwt_enabled required booleans.

steps.name: set-stage-active-deployment
steps._note: setstageactivedeployment (StageSetActiveDeploymentRequest): deployment_id required.

steps.name: create-auth
steps._note: createauth (AuthCreateRequest): returns only $.access_token (NO id) -> auth_id recovered via listauths below. connected_stages references the `dev` stage created by the deployment; expiration_period in days.

steps.name: find-auth-id
steps._note: listauths $.auths[0].id -> auth_id for set/delete (createauth has no id in its response).

steps.name: set-auth
steps._note: setauth (AuthSetRequest): connected_stages required.

steps.name: create-access-control
steps._note: createaccesscontrols (AccessControlCreateRequest): returns $.id directly. allowed_ip_addr CIDR list; connected_stages references the `dev` stage.

steps.name: set-access-control
steps._note: setaccesscontrol (AccessControlSetRequest): allowed_ip_addr + connected_stages required.

steps.name: set-resource-policy
steps._note: setresourcepolicy (ResourcePolicySetRequest): policy_document shape mirrors the base lifecycle's proven shape. The example srn references a non-existent api/method so this may 4xx, but it CALLs setresourcepolicy for coverage.

steps.name: create-usage-plan
steps._note: createusageplan (UsagePlanCreateRequest): returns $.usage_plan.id. rate enum hour|day|month; limit 1..2000000000.

steps.name: set-usage-plan-quota
steps._note: setusageplanquota (UsagePlanSetQuotaRequest): limit + rate required.

steps.name: create-api-key
steps._note: createapikey (ApiKeyCreateRequest): returns $.api_key.id.

lifecycles._note: Light, self-contained PRIVATELINK-ENDPOINT lifecycle covering all 7 privatelink-endpoint write ops (POST create, PUT set, PUT request, PUT connection, PUT approval, DELETE) + the {id} GET via probe-reads. createprivatelinkendpoint needs a real privatelink_service_id which this account may not have, so the whole flow is one optional group `xcov-pl`: a 4xx on create records the CALL for createprivatelinkendpoint and skips the rest cleanly. The set/request/connection/approval PUTs and DELETE are independent standalone groups that run against a synthetic endpoint id (00000000000000000000000000000000) so they are CALLED for coverage even when create 4xxs (expected 400/403/404). Bodies from docs models: privatelinkendpointcreaterequest {name,privatelink_service_id,description}, privatelinkendpointsetrequest {description}, privatelinkendpointrequestrequest {type: CANCEL|RE_REQUEST}. connect/approval request models were not in the docs models dump -> sent as {} (best-effort) with a broad expect_status; PROVENANCE from docs, NOT runtime-proven. NOT heavy: a privatelink endpoint is a control-plane object (no per-hour VM/DB billing) and create is expected to 4xx without a service id, so it stays enabled+light.

steps.name: create-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: createprivatelinkendpoint: privatelink_service_id is a synthetic id (no real PrivateLink service on this account) -> expected 400/404 but CALLs the POST. Returns $.privatelink_endpoint.id on success.

steps.name: set-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: setprivatelinkendpoint against the captured (or, if create 4xxd, the unfilled placeholder) endpoint id -> expected 404, CALLs the PUT for coverage.

steps.name: request-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: requestprivatelinkendpoint (PrivateLinkEndpointRequestRequest): type CANCEL|RE_REQUEST. Targets the captured endpoint id, or the unfilled {privatelink_endpoint_id} placeholder when create 4xxd -> expected 404.

steps.name: connect-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: connectprivatelinkendpoint: request model not in docs dump -> empty body (best-effort). Targets the captured endpoint id, or the unfilled {privatelink_endpoint_id} placeholder when create 4xxd -> expected 404.

steps.name: approve-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: approveprivatelinkendpoint: request model not in docs dump -> empty body (best-effort). Targets the captured endpoint id, or the unfilled {privatelink_endpoint_id} placeholder when create 4xxd -> expected 404.

steps.name: delete-privatelink-endpoint-synthetic
steps._note: deleteprivatelinkendpoint against the captured (or, if create 4xxd, the unfilled placeholder) endpoint id -> expected 404, CALLs the DELETE for coverage regardless of whether create succeeded (uses the captured id or the unfilled placeholder).
